sections.title: Basic Profile & Identity
sections.description: Your personal details and current educational background.

questions.label: What is your full legal name?
questions.placeholder: Enter your full legal name exactly as on your passport

questions.label: What is your date of birth?
What is your current age?
questions.description: Pakistani students must be at least 6 years old for Australia student visa

questions.label: What is your country of residence?
questions.placeholder: e.g., Pakistan, India, China

questions.label: What is your current education level?
questions.description: This helps us understand your educational background

questions.label: What languages do you speak fluently?
questions.placeholder: e.g., English, French, Mandarin

sections.title: Study Plans & Academic Goals
sections.description: Your intended degree, field of study, and academic goals in the Australia.

questions.label: What degree are you planning to pursue?
What is your major or field of study?
questions.placeholder: e.g., Computer Science, Business Administration, Engineering

questions.label: Why did you choose this field of study?
questions.placeholder: Explain your passion, interests, and reasons for choosing this field

questions.label: Tell us about your previous education
questions.placeholder: Your schooling history, and how it prepared you for your intended studies

questions.label: How does this course fit your long-term career goals?
questions.placeholder: Explain how this specific program will help you achieve your career objectives

questions.label: How does your program prepare you for professional practice or registration in your home country or internationally?
questions.placeholder: Describe professional qualifications, accreditations, or certifications this program provides

questions.label: Why didn't you choose a similar course in your home country?
questions.placeholder: Compare the Australian program with available options in your home country. Explain what Australia uniquely offers (curriculum, facilities, faculty, research opportunities, etc.)

sections.title: University & Program Details
sections.description: Information about the universities you've applied to and admitted into.

questions.label: What is the name of your primary university?
questions.placeholder: e.g., University of Australia, Australian National University, University of Melbourne

questions.label: In which city is your university located?
questions.placeholder: e.g., Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide

questions.label: Why did you choose this primary university over others?
questions.placeholder: Explain what makes this university unique for you (program strength, professors, location, rankings, etc.)

questions.label: What do you know about your university's program?
questions.placeholder: Demonstrate research about the program curriculum

questions.label: What subjects/modules are included in your course?
questions.placeholder: Describe the core modules, electives, and practical components of your program. Research your actual course structure.

sections.title: Academic Capability & English Proficiency
sections.description: Your test scores, GPA, and English language capabilities.

questions.label: Which English proficiency test did you take?
questions.description: Australia universities typically require PTE or IELTS

questions.label: What is your PTE score?
questions.placeholder: PTE is out of 90

questions.label: What is your IELTS score?
questions.placeholder: IELTS is out of 9.0

questions.label: What was your previous GPA or academic percentage?
Was your previous education in English?
questions.description: Whether your schooling was conducted in English medium

questions.label: What skills will you gain from this program?
questions.placeholder: Describe technical skills specific to your field and transferable soft skills (communication, problem-solving, teamwork, etc.)

sections.title: Financial Support & Proof of Funds
sections.description: Critical: Your financial sponsor, income, and proof of funds.

questions.label: Who is your primary financial sponsor?
questions.description: Who will support your education financially?

questions.label: What is your sponsor's full name?
What is your relationship to the sponsor?
questions.placeholder: e.g., Father, Mother, Elder Brother

questions.label: What is your sponsor's occupation?
questions.placeholder: e.g., Software Engineer, Business Owner, Government Official

questions.label: What is your sponsor's annual income (in USD)?
questions.description: Annual income in USD (use current exchange rate if needed)

questions.label: Do you have proof of your sponsor's income? (documents available)
questions.description: Tax returns, salary certificates, business documents, etc.

questions.label: What is the total estimated cost of your program?
How much funding can you access for your education?
questions.description: Total available funds (savings + sponsor support) in USD

questions.label: Do you have proof of available funds? (documents)
questions.description: Bank statements, affidavits of support, investment documents

questions.label: Did you receive a scholarship?
What is your scholarship amount (in USD)?
questions.placeholder: Annual or total amount

questions.label: What type of scholarship did you receive?
What if your sponsor cannot support you fully?
questions.placeholder: Describe alternative funding sources or plans (loans, gifts, part-time work, etc.)

questions.label: Are you contributing any personal savings? If yes, what is the source?
questions.placeholder: e.g., Part-time job savings, family gifts, personal work income

questions.label: Do you plan to work part-time while studying? How will you balance it with your studies?
questions.placeholder: Explain your work plans (if any) and how you'll manage time between work and studies. Note: Australia student visa allows up to 20 hours/week during term time.

sections.title: Ties to Home Country & Intent to Return
sections.description: CRITICAL: Your plans after graduation and commitment to return home.

questions.label: Do you plan to return to your home country after graduation?
questions.description: Australia student visa requires demonstrated intent to return home after studies

questions.label: Why will you return to your home country?
questions.placeholder: Family ties, job prospects, career plans, home country opportunities

questions.label: What are your career goals after graduation?
questions.placeholder: Your job targets, industry, company type, location

questions.label: Do you have job prospects or opportunities in your home country?
questions.placeholder: e.g., Family business, job offers, sector growth

questions.label: Do you have close family members in your home country?
questions.description: Family ties are important indicators of home country status

questions.label: Describe your family situation in your home country
questions.placeholder: Parents, siblings, dependents, family business, property

questions.label: Do you have property or assets in your home country?
questions.description: Land, house, business ownership - shows financial ties

questions.label: Are you considering staying in Australia after graduation?
questions.description: Be honest - visa officers appreciate honesty

questions.label: How will you manage healthcare while studying abroad?
questions.placeholder: Discuss healthcare access, insurance, emergency procedures, and any pre-existing health needs

questions.label: What will you do after your visa expires?
questions.placeholder: Describe your plans after your student visa expires - whether returning home, applying for work visas, or other plans. Emphasize your intent and family ties.

sections.title: Travel History & Visa Background
sections.description: Your previous travel and visa application history.

questions.label: Have you visited Australia before?
questions.description: Check if you have any prior travel experience in Australia

questions.label: Do you have any legal issues in Australia?
Have you ever been denied a Australia visa?
questions.description: Check if you have any previous Australia visa refusals

questions.label: Have you ever overstayed a visa in any country?
Do you have any criminal convictions?
Have you been denied a visa to any other country (UK, USA, Canada, etc.)?
Do you understand the conditions of your student visa, including work limitations?
questions.placeholder: Explain your understanding of Australian student visa restrictions on working (e.g., maximum 20 hours/week during term, work eligibility for different sectors)
